business_ideas.idea: Executive health checkups in Bay Area, LA, NYC: 10-20k/year membership, 5000 members = 50M recurring
Marketing lead gen agency (3 dropouts): web, ads, phone answering, $155k/month revenue
Branded fruit stickers for corporate giveaways at $5 per fruit
Bike helmets with cool designs, sold via vintage tricycle stand
Facial masks with user's uploaded face (Mask Alike)
Amazon Go-style convenience store in apartment buildings
Danielle Baskin's 20+ micro-businesses: drones, trading cards of VCs, yoga pitch mats, VR tours, etc
SaaS for witches (Moonlight.world): tarot readings online

predictions.text: Executive health screening will grow as status symbol among wealthy, preventive health is 10-30 year trend

disagreements: Sam and Shaan debate full-body MRI safety: Shaan cautious about endorsing unproven science; Sam cites member success stories from Hampton
dynamics: Sam pitches ideas with enthusiasm; Shaan analyzes business model viability. Multiple tangents into AI music, pizza meter geopolitics, stock exchange business models, and Danielle Baskin's creative micro-business ecosystem. Playful and energetic.
frameworks: Network effects + Enterprise sales + Regulatory defensibility = great business model
Pizza meter: open-source intelligence detects geopolitical signals before news
Big Mac Index: purchasing power parity via commodity pricing
Waffle House Index: hurricane severity via operational status
Big launch fallacy: minimal product launch beats orchestrated big bang
Pivot execution: publicly kill old assumptions before launching new direction
Marketing is tax on unremarkable products: remarkable products market themselves
Membership model beats transactional for predictable recurring revenue
learnings: Preventive health becoming status symbol and competitive advantage among executives
Stock exchanges among most profitable businesses: 60%+ margins, network + regulatory defensibility
Long-term stock exchanges face adoption challenges despite good mission alignment
Early feedback and pivots are part of startup viability testing
Remarkable product design drives word-of-mouth better than marketing spend
Creative micro-entrepreneurs can spin multiple businesses from single core competency
Experiential retail (slime museums) low-labor-intensive, high-margin model
Crypto tokens could have enabled early community to share upside but faced regulatory blockers

banger_quotes.quote: Marketing is the tax you pay for an unremarkable product
Take your old idea and shoot it in the head
Your team will be confused unless you explicitly kill old assumptions

numbers_dropped: Mayo Clinic executive health: $100M+ annual revenue, 177,000 patients in 2015
Stock traders: 10 minutes average holding time
ICE exchange: 60% profit margin, $10B revenue, $35-40B market cap
Slime museums: $6-8M per location, now $30M total revenue across cities
notable: Danielle Baskin's 20+ micro-business universe shows creative generalist can build multiple ventures from taste and design sensibility; story illustrates products remarkability drives growth better than marketing.
